expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the project root
expectations.evidence: outputs/AGENTS.md exists (1509 bytes). Also confirmed at /tmp/eval-legacy-node/AGENTS.md.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md exists and is a symlink pointing to AGENTS.md
knowledge/ directory is created with at least one .md file
expectations.evidence: outputs/knowledge/ contains three .md files: order-lifecycle.md, payment-stripe-integration.md, module-boundaries.md

expectations.text: Knowledge doc filenames describe problem domains (e.g. order-lifecycle.md), not code modules (e.g. service-a.md)
expectations.evidence: Filenames are order-lifecycle.md, payment-stripe-integration.md, module-boundaries.md — all describe business/architectural domains, not code files or modules by file name.

expectations.text: Knowledge docs do not copy implementation details (no function bodies, SQL, or call sequences)
expectations.evidence: All three docs describe rules, constraints, and state transitions in prose/tables. No function bodies or SQL found. payment-stripe-integration.md mentions a call sequence (4-step flow) but at a conceptual level describing the integration pattern, not a code-level call sequence. No raw code or SQL is present.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md contains a table or links pointing to knowledge docs
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md has a markdown table under '## Knowledge Base' with links to all three knowledge docs: knowledge/order-lifecycle.md, knowledge/payment-stripe-integration.md, knowledge/module-boundaries.md.

expectations.text: The 'only webhook sets PAID status' constraint is captured somewhere in the knowledge base
expectations.evidence: Captured in multiple places: (1) AGENTS.md Hard Constraints: '订单状态 PAID 只能由 Stripe Webhook 处理器写入，任何其他路径都是错误的'; (2) order-lifecycle.md state table: 'PAID 支付已确认（仅由 Stripe webhook 设置）'; (3) payment-stripe-integration.md: '订单状态 PAID 只能由 webhook 处理器写入，任何其他路径都是错误的' and Hard Constraints: '不得在 webhook 处理之外手动将订单状态设为 PAID'.

claims.claim: Knowledge docs capture known TODOs in the codebase (unimplemented time-limit check, missing refund status update, missing user module)
claims.evidence: order-lifecycle.md notes two TODOs; payment-stripe-integration.md flags missing refund webhook handling; module-boundaries.md flags missing src/user/ directory and src/db.js.

claims.claim: AGENTS.md includes task routing hints to guide agents to the right document
claims.evidence: AGENTS.md '## Task Routing' section maps four task types to specific knowledge docs.

claims.claim: The knowledge base covers module boundaries and cross-module dependency direction
claims.evidence: module-boundaries.md describes order->payment and payment->order (unidirectional) call relationships explicitly.

suggestions.assertion: Knowledge docs do not copy implementation details (no function bodies, SQL, or call sequences)
suggestions.reason: The assertion checks for absence of raw code but does not verify that docs contain genuinely useful constraint-level information. A doc with no code but nothing meaningful would still pass. Consider: 'Each knowledge doc articulates at least one non-obvious business rule or constraint.'

No assertion checks whether the outputs were placed in the correct project directory (/tmp/eval-legacy-node/) vs. just somewhere. The symlink check partially covers this, but the knowledge/ docs in outputs/ were not verified to also exist in /tmp/eval-legacy-node/knowledge/ — there may be a discrepancy between the workspace copy and the actual project.

suggestions.assertion: AGENTS.md contains a table or links pointing to knowledge docs
suggestions.reason: This assertion is satisfied by any table with any content. It does not verify that the descriptions are accurate or useful. A table with placeholder text would pass.

eval_feedback.overall: All expectations passed with genuine, substantive evidence. The knowledge base is high quality — it captures hard constraints, known TODOs, and architectural invariants without copying raw code. The main eval gap is that assertions check structure/presence but not content quality or whether outputs landed in the actual project directory.
